titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 629*

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=806 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.607604+00 | translation moa=807 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.616379+00

p.cl: Alexander dei gratia Rex scottorum omnibus probis Hominibus Totius terre sue salutem .

concessimus
priori et Monachis de Coldingham dilectis et fidelibus nostris . vt nullus capiat namos suos
uel hominum suorum quos habent in dominico in Baronia de Coldingham pro alicuius debito uel forisfacto
nisi pro eorundem proprio debito uel forisfacto .

Quare firmiter prohibemus ne quis dictos Monachos et homines
suos contra hanc concessionem nostram iniuste uexare presumat super nostram plenariam forisfacturam .

Testibus
W . Comite de Marr’ . Alexandro . senescallo . Dauid de Graham . Nicholao de sules’ . vicecomite de Rokisburc’ . Thoma
filio Ranuli .
apud Coldingham . xxj . aprilis anno regni domini regis xxx . quarto .
Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his land sends greeting.

I have granted to the prior and monks of Coldingham, our well-beloved and sworn men, that no one may take poinds from them, or from their men on the demesne of the barony of Coldingham, for any debt or forfeit save for their own debt or forfeit.
Wherefore we forbid that anyone unjustly seek to trouble the said monks and their men against this our grant, on pain of our full forfeiture.
As witnesses: William, earl of Mar; Alexander, steward; David of Graham; Nicholas de Soules, sheriff of Roxburgh; Thomas son of Ranulf.
At Coldingham, 21 April, in the 34th year of the king's reign [1248].
